description: Creates a custom Okta authorization server with scopes (read, write, admin), identity claims (groups, department, employee_type, manager, is_admin), and token policies with risk-profile-driven lifetimes.

risk_profile_effects.standard: Access tokens: 60 min, refresh tokens: 30 days, service tokens: 60 min.
risk_profile_effects.elevated: Access tokens: 30 min, refresh tokens: 14 days, service tokens: 30 min.
risk_profile_effects.strict: Access tokens: 15 min, refresh tokens: 7 days, service tokens: 15 min.

risk_profile.client_description: Your organization's security tier. Controls token lifetimes — stricter profiles issue shorter-lived tokens to reduce the blast radius of token theft.

group_ids.client_description: Map of group names to Okta group IDs. Passed from the groups module — used to scope token policies.

auth_server_name.client_description: Name for your custom authorization server. This appears in the Okta admin console and in issuer URLs.

auth_server_description.client_description: Description for the authorization server displayed in the Okta admin console.

scopes.client_description: Custom scopes to register on the authorization server. Applications request these scopes during authentication to control API access levels.

access_token_lifetime_minutes.client_description: How long access tokens are valid for internal applications (in minutes). Shorter lifetimes are more secure but cause more frequent re-authentication. Overrides the risk_profile default.

refresh_token_lifetime_days.client_description: How long refresh tokens are valid (in days). After expiry, users must fully re-authenticate. Overrides the risk_profile default.

service_token_lifetime_minutes.client_description: How long access tokens are valid for service-to-service (M2M) integrations (in minutes). Overrides the risk_profile default.

outputs.auth_server_issuer: Authorization server issuer URL (used by applications for OIDC discovery)
outputs.scope_ids: Map of scope name to scope ID
